paper.title: Resolving the Young 2 Cygni Run-away Star into a Binary using iLocater

extraction.summary: The paper characterizes 2 Cygni as a young runaway star with a peculiar velocity of 22.9 +/- 2.9 km/s, but does not present it as a Galactic-unbound or HVS candidate. The object is treated as a bound runaway star whose anomalous velocity may arise from binary supernova or dynamical ejection from a young cluster. No other candidate groups are discussed as possibly unbound from the Milky Way.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 2 Cygni system, a young runaway star with peculiar velocity 22.9 +/- 2.9 km/s discussed in the paper background.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly characterizes 2 Cygni as a runaway star, not as a Galactic-unbound or HVS candidate. Its peculiar velocity (22.9 km/s) is described as placing it above the peak in the peculiar-star velocity histogram, but the paper does not claim or assess it as escaping the Milky Way potential. Ordinary runaways are excluded per Stella inclusion rules.

source_refs.context: paper discussion of 2 Cygni as a runaway star with peculiar velocity
